rationale: The dominant ground-truth concern (raised twice by hsliuustc0106 and answered by the author with FLUX.1-dev A/B numbers) is that the platform-wide default change was validated only on Qwen-Image but applied to all CUDA diffusion models. Y explicitly surfaces and investigates this scope question, correctly traces the Cosmos3 override path, and cites the FLUX.1-dev −14.1% latency / SSIM 0.992 figures that match the ground truth's actual resolution almost exactly — strong grounding, not fabrication. X performs similar Cosmos3/scope investigation internally but never surfaces it as a finding, instead producing two low-value 'add a comment' suggestions about an unused parameter and vacuous test parametrization that don't map to any reviewer concern. Neither candidate flags the 'pytest mark not complete' comment, and neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section, so gap_hit is false for both. Y's findings are more grounded and actionable overall, giving it clearly higher recall and precision.
